file_insights.purpose: Global error handler middleware for Express application

patterns_discovered.description: Uses type guard functions (isZodError, isPrismaError, isJsonParseError) to identify and handle different error types appropriately
patterns_discovered.benefit: Centralized error type checking with reusable logic

patterns_discovered.description: Converts different error types (Zod, Prisma, generic Error) into a unified AppException format
patterns_discovered.benefit: Standardizes error handling across the application

patterns_discovered.description: Maps specific Prisma error codes (P2002, P2003, P2025, etc.) to application-specific exceptions
patterns_discovered.benefit: Database-specific error semantics preserved in application layer

patterns_discovered.description: Generates unique request IDs and includes them in error responses for tracking and debugging
Higher-order function that wraps async route handlers to automatically catch and forward errors to middleware
patterns_discovered.benefit: Eliminates try-catch boilerplate in route handlers

patterns_discovered.description: Generic wrapper for promise-based operations with automatic error conversion
patterns_discovered.benefit: Consistent error handling in service layer without explicit try-catch

patterns_discovered.description: Stack traces only included in development environment, hidden in production
patterns_discovered.benefit: Security best practice preventing information disclosure

gotchas_discovered.description: The error type checks must happen in correct order - AppException check before generic Error check, otherwise all errors would match the generic Error instance check
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Type guards ordered from most specific to most generic

gotchas_discovered.description: isPrismaError assumes error.code is a string and checks for 'P' prefix, but Prisma can have other error types; code string checking is crucial
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Type guard includes explicit string type validation

gotchas_discovered.description: Request ID resolution uses three fallbacks (request.id, X-Request-ID header, generated) - ambiguity about which takes precedence
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Fallback chain is reasonable but behavior should be documented

gotchas_discovered.description: Complex conditional logic for including exception details - distinguishes between validation errors array and other details
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Logic is correct but somewhat brittle if exception.details structure changes

gotchas_discovered.description: Error handlers require exactly 4 parameters (including err and unused _next) to be recognized by Express as error middleware
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Signature is correct with explicit unused _next parameter

approach_outcome.summary: Comprehensive global error handler middleware successfully created with 384 lines of well-documented code
key_achievements: Implemented ErrorRequestHandler that conforms to Express middleware pattern
Created type-safe error discrimination system supporting Zod, Prisma, and AppException
Added utility functions (asyncHandler, withErrorBoundary) to simplify error handling in application code
Implemented request correlation through request ID tracking
Environment-aware error logging and stack trace exposure (dev vs production)
approach_outcome.coverage: Handles expected error types: validation errors, database errors, JSON parsing errors, internal errors, and unknown errors

recommendations.recommendation: Create unit tests for error conversion functions (convertZodErrors, convertPrismaError) with specific error code cases
Document the request ID priority/fallback behavior and how to propagate request IDs through service layers
Consider adding error event emission or metrics collection (e.g., error type counts) for production monitoring
Add support for rate limiting errors and authentication/authorization errors with specific handling
Verify errorHandler and notFoundHandler are registered in the correct order in main Express app setup (404 before error handler)
Consider using a discriminated union type for error handling to ensure all error paths are type-checked
